subcommands.name: convene
subcommands.description: Run a panel debate on a topic and persist results to the local DB. For one-shot questions use `council ask`. For conversation use `council chat`.

arguments.name: topic
arguments.description: The topic / question for the panel to debate (optional when --prompt-file is used)

options.description: Read the topic VERBATIM from a file (or `-` for stdin) instead of the positional argument. Bypasses the shell so `$`, backticks, and values like `$180K` survive intact. Mutually exclusive with the positional <topic>.
Use a built-in or custom panel template (alias: --template). **Omit to let Council auto-design an expert panel from your topic.**
Use a built-in or custom panel template (alias: --panel). **Omit to let Council auto-design an expert panel from your topic.**
Expert slugs from the library (space- or comma-separated, repeatable). Bypasses both --template and auto-compose.
Engine to use (default: from config)
Output format (auto picks Ink TUI on TTY, plain text otherwise)
Max rounds (freeform mode only)
Debate mode
Soft per-response word cap
Add a human participant by name (repeatable)
Moderator strategy for freeform mode (round-robin | devils-advocate | consensus-check). devils-advocate accepts an optional ":<slug>" suffix to pin the advocate (defaults to first expert).
Visibility scope for prior turns: all | same-round | recent
Start rolling-summary after N rounds. Omit to disable.
Use simpler local summarizer instead of LLM — for offline/air-gapped use
Skip post-debate LLM extraction — for offline/air-gapped use. Useful for offline tests and air-gapped environments.
Skip the auto-compose confirmation prompt — required for non-interactive / CI runs
Skip automatic conclusion synthesis after a completed debate
Show template migration notices and zero-change summaries
Suppress informational output
Model to use for experts (default: from config; run 'council doctor --models' to list available models)
Maximum number of experts for auto-compose

arguments.name: panel

options.description: Output format

Engine for continue mode

Moderator strategy for --prompt freeform mode (round-robin | devils-advocate | consensus-check). devils-advocate accepts an optional ":<slug>" suffix.
Skip post-debate LLM extraction — for offline/air-gapped use. Useful for offline tests and air-gapped environments.
Resume the most recent panel session

subcommands.name: conclude
subcommands.description: Synthesize a panel's most substantive debate into a structured decision framework. For transcript-based ADR (Architecture Decision Record) export, use `council export --format adr` instead.

arguments.name: panel
arguments.description: Panel name to conclude (defaults to the most recently created panel)

options.description: Engine kind (default: from config)
Output format
Synthesis timeout in milliseconds
Model to use for synthesis (default: from config)

subcommands.name: review
subcommands.description: Run the built-in code-review expert panel over a diff and print the review. The diff is SENT to the configured AI engine (e.g. Copilot); use --engine mock to run offline.

options.description: Read the unified diff from a file, or `-` to read stdin. When omitted, Council reviews `git diff <base>` (your local changes).
Git ref to diff the working tree against when --diff-file is omitted
Engine to use (default: from config)
Output format
Max debate rounds (default: the code-review panel's own default)
Soft per-response word cap

subcommands.name: ask
subcommands.description: Ask one expert from an existing panel a single question. For multi-expert debates use `council convene`. For conversation use `council chat`.

arguments.name: panel
arguments.description: Panel name from a previous debate (as shown by `council sessions`). For library experts, use `council chat`.

arguments.name: question
arguments.description: The question to ask (optional when --prompt-file is used)

options.description: Read the question VERBATIM from a file (or `-` for stdin) instead of the positional argument. Bypasses the shell so `$`, backticks, and values like `$180K` survive intact. Mutually exclusive with the positional <question>.
Engine to use (default: from config)
Expert slug to ask (default: first expert in the panel)
Output format
Soft per-response word cap

subcommands.description: Persistent conversation with an expert or panel from the library. For structured debates use `council convene`.

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Expert slug or panel name to chat with

options.description: Engine to use (default: from config)
